Game Drama Score.details: Decided by 38 points with 4 lead changes and 8 ties. The game saw 1 major win probability swing, and the losing team's chances peaked at 48%.

Most Valuable Play.details: Q1, 1:25, Green (DAL): Green Tip Dunk Shot (2 PTS). It swung the win probability by +10.6 points.

Most Damaging Play.details: Q1, 6:50, Irving (DAL): Irving 3' Driving Layup (4 PTS). It swung the win probability by -9.1 points.

Clutch-Time Scoring Summary.value: Scoring over the final five minutes: home team 7, away team 12.

recap.Biggest Turning Point: The game turned at Q1, 6:14: Lively II 3PT Jump Shot (3 PTS) (Doncic 1 AST). That single play moved DAL's chance of winning from 51% to 60%. No other moment shifted the game more, and it went DAL's way.
recap.Player Impact: No one influenced the outcome more than Dončić of DAL. Across 49 plays, his actions moved the win probability by a combined 37 percentage points, the most of any player on the floor.
recap.Comeback Reality: The tensest moment for a comeback came at Q4, 5:58, with BOS down 48 points. The model put their chance of pulling it off at 0%. In other words: nearly impossible, but not nothing.
recap.Hidden Momentum: The strongest run that didn't fully show on the scoreboard came at Q1, 0:29.4, with the game tilting DAL's way. The play in the middle of it: Washington 25' 3PT Running Jump Shot (3 PTS) (Irving 2 AST).
recap.Model Note: These numbers come from ClutchCast's champion model, the winner of a competition between six models. The winner is judged on how honest its probabilities are on games it never trained on, not just how often it picks the right winner.
